meta.name: PMS consistency conventions
meta.note: Publicly-established engineering conventions only (standard class series, facings, physical monotonicity). No copyrighted rating values. Message templates must stay byte-identical to the legacy pmskit.validate output while the shadow-parity gate is active.
meta.context_fields: Engine-derived per class: derived.flange_class_number (int|null, from CL.### in flange_rating_face), derived.facing (RTJ/FFS/RJ/RF/FF/MFF/LMF/SMF|null), derived.has_cl_token (bool), derived.pt_len_equal (bool|null when either array empty), derived.press_monotonic_violation (bool). Per component (scope=component): comp.part, comp.description, derived.component_class_number (int|null).

rules.source: ASME B16.5 / B16.1 class series (public fact)
rules.message: Flange class CL{derived.flange_class_number} is not a standard ASME B16.5/B16.1 class
No standard facing (RF/FF/RTJ...) found in flange rating

rules.source: industry convention (guidance, not a hard rule)
rules.message: RTJ facing is unusual below Class 600 (here CL{derived.flange_class_number})

rules.source: industry convention (guidance, not a hard rule)
rules.message: Flat-face (FF) is unusual at Class {derived.flange_class_number} and above
Corrosion allowance not captured for this class

rules.source: physics: rated pressure must not increase with temperature
rules.message: Allowable pressure increases with temperature (check parse/data)

rules.source: cross-check: component CL vs class flange CL (800 allowed: SW valves)
rules.message: {comp.part} references CL{derived.component_class_number} vs class flange CL{derived.flange_class_number}
Class has no parsed component rows
